response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and the Green Map GIS application, and demonstrated ability to build high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the role's primary technical requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and Bogdan's career stack is overwhelmingly JavaScript, TypeScript, and Node.js. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project using C++ for audio synthesis — neither reflects the depth of modern systems-level C++ required for production routing tile pipelines. There is no Rust experience mentioned, no background in navigation guidance or routing data systems, and no evidence of working with geospatial tile formats or pipeline latency optimization at the required scale.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS, including a FOSS4G conference talk on open geo-data tooling
Demonstrated high-performance distributed systems work at Dunnhumby with async I/O, low-latency apps, and microservices at millions-of-requests-per-second scale
Strong Linux background with practical experience in containerized, distributed infrastructure including Kubernetes and Docker
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience — the role's core technical requirement; existing C++ work is from 2014–2015 and a thesis project, not production systems engineering
Entire recent career (2018–present) is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js with no systems-level programming, disqualifying for a C++-primary role
Zero background in routing, navigation guidance, or routing tile pipelines — the specific domain this role operates in

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation domain knowledge
